chunks.text: MAK/PS ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH KARACHI Const. Petition No. S-1168 of 2025 __________________________________________________________________ Date Order with signature(s) of Judge(s) Hearing of Case. 1. For orders on office objections. 2. For hearing of main case. 17.11.2025. Mr. Ashfaq Ahmed Shah, Advocate for the Petitioner. Mr. Ali Zardari, Assistant Advocate General Sindh. Mr. Wajid A. Majeed, Advocate for Respondent No.8 Ms. Rahat Ahsan, DPG ASI Dilnawaz, PS Makali, District Thatta. ------------------------------------ Learned counsel for the petitioner through instant petition seeks following relief:- A. To Direct the Respondent No. 2 to pass directions to his subordinates police officials not lodged any false and bogus FIR against the petitioner and his Family Members on the instigation of Respondent NO. 6 to 8. B. To direct the Respondent No. 2 to produce if any FIR is registered at any police station in Sindh province and may not arrest the petitioner or any other family members. C. To direct the Respondent No. 1 to make ensure to protect the fundamental rights of petitioner and no further FIR's lodged anywhere in the limits of Pakistan. D. To direct the Respondent No. 6 to provide the protection the repetitions and family members. E. To direct the Private Respondent No. 5 & 6 not be caused any harassment or lodge any false FIR through these agents against the petitioner and family members. F. Grant any other relief deemed just and proper in the circumstances of the case, in the interest of justice. Pursuant to court notices official respondents have filed their comments, which are taken on record, wherein it is undertaken that no harassment would be caused to the petitioner.

Grant any other relief deemed just and proper in the circumstances of the case, in the interest of justice. Pursuant to court notices official respondents have filed their comments, which are taken on record, wherein it is undertaken that no harassment would be caused to the petitioner. Mr. Ghulam Rasool Sammon, advocate files Vakalatnama on behalf of Respondent No.6 & 7 and state at bar that respondent No.6 & 7 will not cause any harassment to the petitioner. Learned AAG and DPG submits that in terms of prayer clause official respondents shall conduct themselves strictly in accordance with law and no harassment would be caused to the petitioner. Counsel for the petitioner seeks disposal of the instant petition in view of the reply filed by the official respondents and statement made by the AAG & DPG. Order accordingly. This petition stands disposed of in the above terms alongwith pending applications. A copy of this order shall be sent to SSP District Thatta for compliance. JUDGE
